description: Elicitation establishes where the application will run before any stack is committed, and the stack the tool builds is constrained by that answer, so the owner is not led into a stack their hosting cannot run.
iWant: the tool to work out where my app will run before it picks the technology to build it with
soThat: it does not build me something my hosting cannot actually run
title: Deploy target is established before the stack is chosen

acceptanceCriteria.description: Elicitation asks where the app will run as an early item before any stack is committed
acceptanceCriteria.when: an agent runs elicitation with an owner
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance places a deploy-target question early, before any technology stack is committed

acceptanceCriteria.description: The stack choice is constrained by the deploy-target answer
acceptanceCriteria.when: the agent chooses the technology stack
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance requires the stack to be constrained to what that target can host, so an incompatible stack is not committed

acceptanceCriteria.description: The deploy target and its stack implication are captured as an ADR on the project tree
acceptanceCriteria.given: a deploy target established during elicitation
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance requires it and the stack constraint it implies to be recorded as an ADR on the project's own tree, visible and revisable

acceptanceCriteria.description: The harness fragment forbids committing a stack before the deploy target is established
acceptanceCriteria.given: the harness fragment written into agent-instructions files
acceptanceCriteria.then: it carries a rule that a technology stack must not be committed before the deploy target is established
